mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.4 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive in > 55% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when UNRATE changes are below the 0.4pp threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real GDP and retail sales indicates synchronized domestic demand growth that boosts corporate earnings forecasts, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, or 60-day forward returns are negative in > 60% of periods where both GDPC1 and RSAFS show positive quarter-over-quarter growth.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production trigger immediate supply-side risk aversion among equity investors, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant negative correlation with INDPRO declines during recovery phases, or the mechanism reverses direction during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price index levels combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicate tightening real monetary conditions that compress equity multiples, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL increases > 0.3% month-over-month while FEDFUNDS does not decline
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL rises and FEDFUNDS holds steady, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 2-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show zero or negative correlation with PAYEMS growth, or the signal fails to predict returns during periods of productivity-driven employment shifts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in housing starts below 500,000 units signals collapsing construction demand and negative wealth effects, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in > 50% of instances where HOUST drops below 500,000, or the threshold effect disappears when controlling for interest rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising unemployment and falling industrial production indicates a supply-demand mismatch that forces corporate margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases while INDPRO declines month-over-month
failure_condition: The divergence signal fails to predict negative returns, or 20-day forward returns remain positive when UNRATE rises and INDPRO falls, indicating the mechanism breaks down under monetary accommodation.
